projects.category: iOS App · Live on App Store

why.en: Built for my newborn daughter. New parents face a flood of contradictory guidance around introducing solids — what's safe, what's age-appropriate, how to handle allergens. I wanted one calm, evidence-informed tool that answered those questions without adding anxiety.
why.es: Creado para mi hija recién nacida. Los padres primerizos enfrentan una avalancha de información contradictoria sobre la introducción de sólidos.
why.de: Für meine neugeborene Tochter gebaut. Neue Eltern sind mit widersprüchlichen Ratschlägen zur Beikost überfordert.

result.en: Live on the App Store. Features a full food database with safety ratings, allergen tracking, age-appropriate guidance and a feeding log — built solo in SwiftUI from research through to App Store submission.
result.es: Disponible en el App Store. Base de datos de alimentos, seguimiento de alérgenos, guía apropiada por edad y registro de alimentación.
result.de: Im App Store. Lebensmitteldatenbank, Allergen-Tracking, altersgerechte Hinweise und Ernährungsprotokoll.

points: Scoping a solo build forces brutal prioritisation — I defined the MVP around the core anxiety (what can my baby eat right now?) and deferred everything else to v2.
Without a client or team, scope creep is self-inflicted; writing a one-page brief before each sprint kept me accountable to the original problem.
Designing for sleep-deprived, one-handed parents meant ruthlessly simplifying every screen — clarity always beat completeness.
Real user testing with new mums surfaced navigation patterns I'd never have anticipated from behind a desk.
Healthcare-adjacent apps need a calm, trustworthy aesthetic — white space, soft palettes and consistent iconography carry more emotional weight than animation.
Accessibility constraints (dynamic type, high contrast) weren't an afterthought; building for them first made the design cleaner for everyone.
The biggest challenge was information architecture — parents need safety reassurance first, food discovery second. Restructuring the hierarchy around that insight reduced early drop-off.
Onboarding had to earn trust fast; showing the evidence basis for each recommendation within the first two screens changed the tone of the whole experience.
Building a CoreData-backed food database in SwiftUI early in my iOS journey taught me that schema decisions at day 1 are expensive to change post-launch — model first, build second.
App Store submission involves more product thinking than I expected: screenshot composition, metadata copywriting and review guidelines are all part of the shipped product.

projects.category: iOS App · Live on App Store

why.en: As a coach and exercise scientist, I used interval timers constantly — and every existing app was either too cluttered or too simple. I wanted one that felt as clean as the training it was built for.
why.es: Como entrenador y científico del ejercicio, usaba temporizadores de intervalos constantemente — y cada app era demasiado recargada o demasiado simple.
why.de: Als Trainer und Sportwissenschaftler nutzte ich ständig Intervall-Timer — jede bestehende App war entweder überladen oder zu simpel.

result.en: Live on the App Store. Clean interval timer with customisable work/rest cycles, sound and haptic cues, background accuracy and a home screen widget — designed and built end-to-end.
result.es: Disponible en el App Store. Temporizador limpio con ciclos personalizables, señales sonoras, precisión en segundo plano y widget.
result.de: Im App Store. Intervall-Timer mit anpassbaren Intervallen, Sound-Signalen, Hintergrundgenauigkeit und Widget.

points: Interval timers sound deceptively simple — defining edge cases (rest periods, sound cues, haptics, background state, widget) revealed how much scope hides in 'simple' utility apps.
Positioning matters even in a crowded category; understanding why existing apps frustrated coaches helped me write a focused App Store description that converted.

The design challenge was making complex timer configurations feel instant and intuitive — progressive disclosure (simple defaults, advanced settings tucked away) is the right pattern for utility apps.
Designing the widget forced me to think about the absolute minimum information a user needs at a glance — a useful constraint that improved the main UI too.
High contrast, large touch targets and readable countdown typography matter enormously when users are mid-exercise and glancing at the screen for half a second.
State transitions (work → rest → done) needed to be immediately distinguishable by colour and sound — not just label text.
User testing mid-workout revealed that auditory and haptic feedback mattered far more than visual design — restructured the whole alert system around sound and haptic first.
The biggest UX failure in competing apps was buried settings; I surfaced the three most-changed values (work time, rest time, rounds) directly on the main screen.

iOS background timer accuracy is non-trivial — the OS throttles background tasks in ways that affect UX. Learned to use BackgroundModes, UserNotifications and local notification scheduling to keep timing accurate.
WidgetKit has a different mental model to SwiftUI views; understanding the timeline-based refresh system required rethinking how I structured the app's shared state.

why.en: Built to share practical movement education beyond 1:1 sessions — reaching people who want to train smarter, prevent injury and build sustainable habits. The brand spans a coaching website, YouTube channel and social content.
why.es: Creado para compartir educación práctica del movimiento más allá de las sesiones individuales.
why.de: Aufgebaut um praktische Bewegungsbildung über Einzelsitzungen hinaus zu teilen.

result.en: A live coaching website (movementbydesign.es) built and optimised end-to-end, plus a YouTube and Instagram brand — managing positioning, content strategy, video production, SEO and audience growth from scratch.
result.es: Un sitio web de coaching en vivo (movementbydesign.es) construido y optimizado, más una marca en YouTube e Instagram.
result.de: Eine live Coaching-Website (movementbydesign.es) von Grund auf aufgebaut, plus YouTube- und Instagram-Marke.

why.en: Exercise therapy in clinical oncology is underutilised and poorly coordinated. I joined Goethe University Frankfurt to contribute to research that aimed to change that — building the evidence base and practical delivery frameworks that make exercise part of real cancer care.
why.es: La terapia de ejercicio en oncología clínica está infrautilizada. Me uní a la Universidad Goethe de Frankfurt para contribuir a la investigación.
why.de: Bewegungstherapie in der klinischen Onkologie wird zu wenig genutzt. Ich trat der Goethe-Universität Frankfurt bei.

result.en: Contributed to multiple peer-reviewed studies — including the EXERCISE-CC randomised controlled trial and the MOVE-ONKO multi-clinic implementation study — both published in international journals.
result.es: Contribuí a múltiples estudios revisados por pares, incluyendo EXERCISE-CC y MOVE-ONKO.
result.de: Beiträge zu mehreren peer-reviewed Studien, einschließlich EXERCISE-CC und MOVE-ONKO.

abstracts.title: MOVE-ONKO
abstracts.subtitle: Multi-professional Care Pathway for Exercise Therapy in Oncology
abstracts.summary: Evaluates the implementation of a coordinated care network across three major German regions connecting cancer patients with quality-assured, needs-oriented exercise therapy. The study developed a structured pathway between oncology centres and local exercise providers, supported by a digital platform for patient-professional communication. Funded by German Cancer Aid (Deutsche Krebshilfe), the project ran across multiple Comprehensive Cancer Centres from 2023.

abstracts.title: EXERCISE-CC
abstracts.subtitle: Exercise Early to Reduce Corticosteroid-Induced Side Effects in Childhood Cancer — RCT
abstracts.summary: An 8-week randomised controlled trial investigating whether structured exercise, initiated within the first weeks of treatment, can reduce corticosteroid-induced complications (muscle weakness, bone loss, metabolic changes) in children and adolescents recently diagnosed with leukemia or lymphoma. Co-designed and delivered at Goethe University Frankfurt as part of the clinical research team.

papers.title: MOVE-ONKO: Multi-professional care pathway for exercise therapy in oncological patients
EXERCISE-CC: Exercise early to reduce corticosteroid-induced side effects in childhood cancer — RCT Protocol
